title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Dermatitis
intro: Herbal teas for dermatitis are a type of natural remedy that involves steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing and calming tea, which is then consumed to alleviate symptoms of dermatitis. These teas can treat dermatitis by reducing inflammation, calming the skin, and promoting healing due to the presence of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and other beneficial properties found in the herbs. Some examples of herbal teas that are commonly used to treat dermatitis include calendula tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling, chamomile tea, which can calm the skin and promote relaxation, and peppermint tea, which can help to reduce itching and soothe the skin. Other herbal teas, such as green tea, lavender tea, and tea tree oil-infused tea, also have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to heal and protect the skin from further irritation.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas are a natural remedy used to treat dermatitis, a skin condition characterized by inflammation and irritation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Aloe barbadensis teas help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dermatitis. The bioactive constituents present in Aloe barbadensis teas, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E, work together to soothe and calm the skin, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection. By incorporating Aloe barbadensis teas into a treatment plan, individuals can experience a range of benefits, including reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and boil it in a pot.
Take 2-3 tablespoons of fresh Aloe barbadensis gel from the leaf and cut it into small pieces.
Add the Aloe gel pieces to the boiling water and reduce the heat to a simmer.
Let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain it into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the Aloe tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm the skin from dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Infectious disorders drug targets", Aloe barbadensis teas for dermatitis is not specifically mentioned in the given study, however, the study does mention that Aloe is a therapeutically active natural herbal resource that is potent and safe in the treatment of dermatological infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be an effective treatment for dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Aloe barbadensis teas, including avoiding consumption in large quantities, monitoring blood sugar levels, and being cautious when applying the gel topically to sensitive skin areas to minimize irritation and allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Wash affected area with mild soap and lukewarm water.
Dampen affected area with distilled water.
Apply 5 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel directly to affected area.
Soak in warm bath with 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis tea for 15-20 minutes.
Repeat application and bath soak 2-3 times daily for 7-10 days.

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, a chronic inflammatory skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and skin irritation. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of calendula teas help to calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dermatitis. The bioactive constituents, including triterpenoids, carotenoids, and flavonoids, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to the treatment of this ailment. The benefits of calendula teas in treating dermatitis include improved skin hydration, reduced inflammation, and accelerated wound healing, making them a popular herbal remedy for skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried flowers for every 1 cup of boiling water. You can adjust the amount to your liking.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Remove the pot from the heat and add the 1 tablespoon of dried flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for skin relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology", Calendula officinalis teas for dermatitis showed that it promoted wound healing in human umbilical vein endothelial cells (HUVEC) and exhibited anti-inflammatory effects when used in a three-herbs formula with Cortex Moutan and Herba Menthae.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be effective in treating dermatitis, but some possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, which may worsen the condition. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to use high-quality Calendula officinalis teas, start with small doses, and monitor your skin's response closely, avoiding prolonged exposure to sunlight and extreme temperatures.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in 1 cup of warm Calendula tea.
Apply the cloth to affected skin for 15-20 minutes, 3 times a day.
Drink 1 cup of Calendula tea, 2-3 times a day, for internal relief.
Use 2 tablespoons of Calendula cream or ointment, 2-3 times a day, for skin moisturizing.
Continue usage for 2-4 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of nettle tea, including flavonoids, polyphenols, and triterpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dermatitis. By consuming nettle tea, individuals can benefit from its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the symptoms of dermatitis, and promoting overall skin health. The benefits of using nettle tea to treat dermatitis include reduced inflammation, improved skin hydration, and a decrease in the severity and frequency of symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers. Wash them in cold water.
Dry the leaves and flowers using a towel or a salad spinner.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Urtica dioica and put it in a tea infuser.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the tea infuser. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Urtica dioica teas, based on the extract's anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidant properties, may be beneficial in treating dermatitis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used as a potential treatment for dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions should be taken when using Urtica dioica teas to treat dermatitis, including avoiding consumption in large quantities, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies, as it may exacerbate existing conditions.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Urtica dioica teas for dermatitis treatment.
Drink 1/2 cup of Urtica dioica tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks.
Take 1 teaspoon of Urtica dioica powder 2-3 times a day with 1 cup of water.
Monitor skin condition and adjust dosage as recommended by your doctor.
Combine Urtica dioica tea with other natural remedies as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort tea, have been used to treat dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea helps to treat dermatitis by reducing redness and itching, promoting wound healing, and preventing infection. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, in Hypericum perforatum teas contribute to their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and protecting the skin from oxidative stress. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat dermatitis include reduced inflammation, accelerated wound healing, and improved skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Hypericum perforatum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, for a sweetener.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from dermatitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Hypericum perforatum teas for dermatitis may be beneficial due to its antibacterial and antifungal effects, as well as its wound-healing properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but its use may be associated with possible side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications that may exacerbate the condition. To use Hypericum perforatum teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increase as needed, and monitor for any adverse reactions, especially in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and those with a history of kidney or liver problems.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Hypericum perforatum tea for dermatitis treatment.
Take 1-2 cups of Hypericum perforatum tea 3 times a day for 2-3 weeks.
Use a compress soaked in cooled tea to apply directly to affected skin.
Continue treatment for 2-4 weeks or as recommended by your doctor.
Monitor skin condition and adjust treatment plan if symptoms persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of milk thistle tea, including silymarin, flavonoids, and ascorbic acid, help to reduce inflammation, promote skin regeneration, and protect against oxidative stress, ultimately alleviating the symptoms of dermatitis. By modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation, milk thistle tea can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with dermatitis. The benefits of using milk thistle tea to treat dermatitis include reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and a decreased risk of complications, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried flowers and place them in a clean tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the tea infuser.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink 2-3 times a day for relief from dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Silybum marianum teas safely, it's essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, be cautious of interactions with other medications, and avoid using it for extended periods of time.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for dermatitis.
Drink 2 cups of cooled Silybum marianum tea 3 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of Silybum marianum tincture 3 times a day.
Continue treatment for 2-3 weeks or as advised by your doctor.
Monitor your condition and adjust dosage if symptoms persist or worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and swelling. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, caffeic acid, and chicoric acid, in Echinacea purpurea teas have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits in treating dermatitis. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can experience relief from dermatitis symptoms, such as reduced itching and inflammation, and promote overall skin health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 tablespoon of Echinacea purpurea flowers and place them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe and calm skin irritation from dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be effective in treating dermatitis, but potential side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, as well as gastrointestinal issues, like nausea or stomach upset. To minimize side effects, individuals should start with low doses, monitor their body's response, and avoid taking the teas if they experience any severe or persistent symptoms, and also be aware that long-term use may lead to interactions with other medications.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea per cup of boiling water.
Continue using tea for 2-6 weeks for optimal results.
Monitor skin condition and adjust tea usage as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Chamomilla recutita teas have been traditionally used to treat various skin issues, including dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with dermatitis. The bioactive constituents, including apigenin and luteolin, in Chamomilla recutita teas have been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects in treating dermatitis. By incorporating Chamomilla recutita teas into a treatment regimen, individuals may experience relief from the symptoms of dermatitis, such as itching and skin lesions, and may also enjoy improved skin health and a reduced risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Chamomilla recutita flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried flowers in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help soothe skin irritations from dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Chamomilla recutita teas for dermatitis have been found to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal effects, anti-inflammatory properties, and wound-healing properties, making them a promising candidate in the treatment of skin diseases in dogs.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Chamomilla recutita teas can be an effective treatment for dermatitis, but its use may be associated with side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and skin irritation in some individuals. Precautions when using Chamomilla recutita teas for dermatitis treatment include avoiding excessive consumption, as it may cause stomach upset, and being cautious when combining it with other medications, especially blood thinners, to minimize the risk of interactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Chamomilla recutita tea 3 times a day.
Soak affected skin areas in warm Chamomilla recutita tea for 10 minutes.
Use 2 tablespoons of dried Chamomilla recutita in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink Chamomilla recutita tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable improvement.
Consult a doctor for proper diagnosis and treatment of dermatitis.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, a skin condition characterized by inflammation and itching. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis teas help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the symptoms of dermatitis. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that aid in the treatment of dermatitis. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dermatitis include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the risk of secondary infections, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or sugar to taste.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from dermatitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating dermatitis, however, potential side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. When using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dermatitis, it is recommended to start with small dosages and monitor the body's response, also avoiding its consumption during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to the lack of extensive research on its safety during these periods.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Melissa officinalis leaves in 1 cup of boiling water.
Drink 2 cups of the tea, 3 times a day, for 1 week.
After 1 week, reduce to 1 cup, 2 times a day, for maintenance.
Apply cooled tea compress to affected skin, 2-3 times a day.
Monitor skin condition and adjust tea frequency as needed, under doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, also known as dandelion tea, have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with dermatitis. The bioactive constituents, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, in dandelion tea have been found to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits in treating dermatitis. The benefits of using dandelion tea to treat dermatitis include its ability to promote skin healing, reduce itching and discomfort, and provide a natural and non-invasive treatment option for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces using a knife.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it reaches boiling point.
Add 2-3 tablespoons of the chopped Taraxacum officinale to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the Taraxacum officinale. Let the tea cool before drinking 1-2 cups per day.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. When using Taraxacum officinale teas, precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large quantities, as excessive consumption may lead to an imbalance of potassium levels and increased risk of bleeding due to the presence of coumarins, and individuals with kidney or liver disease should use caution.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Taraxacum officinale tea, 3 times a day.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea, 1 tablespoon per cup of water.
Apply the tea-soaked cloth to affected skin for 15-30 minutes.
Repeat the application 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks.
Monitor skin condition and adjust tea dosage or application as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, a skin condition characterized by inflammation and irritation. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of Avena sativa teas help to calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dermatitis. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa teas, including avenanthramides and ferulic acid, contribute to its therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation and protecting the skin from damage. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating dermatitis, including improved skin health, reduced itching and inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa, a strainer, and a cup.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from dermatitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Avena sativa teas may be considered a potential therapeutic option for dermatitis due to its keratolytic and debris-peeling effects, which can help evacuate impurities from the body's excretory system and maintain skin tone consistency.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications due to its sedative properties and potential impact on the digestive system. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it's essential to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it in large quantities or combining it with other medications that slow down breathing or heart rate.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Avena sativa tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1-2 tablespoons of dried Avena sativa as supplement.
Use Avena sativa tea as a compress on affected skin.
Soak in a warm bath with 2 cups of Avena sativa tea.
Repeat usage for 1-2 weeks, then reassess skin condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas, also known as horsetail tea, have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dermatitis by reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals. The bioactive constituents of Equisetum arvense teas, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and silica, contribute to its therapeutic effects, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. Drinking Equisetum arvense tea has been reported to provide benefits in treating dermatitis, including reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense stems and roots. Wash them with clean water.
Dry the stems and roots in a warm place, or use a dehydrator, until they are completely dry.
Grind 2 tablespoons of the dried Equisetum arvense into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture with a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe dermatitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption and potential interactions with other medications, as excessive use may lead to an imbalance of electrolytes and worsen kidney function.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Equisetum arvense tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried herb for each cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes before straining and drinking.
Continue treatment for 2-4 weeks or until symptoms improve.
Consult a healthcare professional for guidance and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been used to treat dermatitis, a skin condition characterized by inflammation and irritation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these teas help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the symptoms of dermatitis such as redness, itching, and swelling. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the skin. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat dermatitis include reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and a decrease in the need for conventional medications, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.

Boil the water in a pot and then reduce the heat to low.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe dermatitis symptoms.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of potassium deficiency due to the tea's licorice root content. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is essential to consume it in moderation, avoid using it for extended periods, and monitor your body's response to the tea, particularly if you have underlying health conditions or are taking medications that may interact with the tea's components.

preparations.herb_desc: Hamamelis virginiana teas, derived from the witch hazel plant, have been used to treat dermatitis, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and skin irritation. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, thereby alleviating symptoms of dermatitis. The bioactive constituents, including tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, have been shown to exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to the therapeutic benefits of Hamamelis virginiana teas in treating dermatitis. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Hamamelis virginiana teas can provide significant relief from the symptoms of dermatitis, promoting skin healing and overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers. You can buy them at a health store or online.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water in a pot and pour it over the dried Hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. This allows the active ingredients to release into the water.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup. Discard the solids.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Hamamelis virginiana tea 2-3 times a day to help with dermatitis symptoms. You can also add honey or lemon to taste.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Forschende Komplementarmedizin, Hamamelis virginiana teas may be of value in selected dermatological indications, although further controlled clinical efficacy studies are needed to confirm its effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hamamelis virginiana teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using this herbal preparation, it's essential to take precautions, such as monitoring for signs of an allergic reaction, starting with a small dose, and avoiding consumption if you have any underlying liver or kidney issues.

preparations.herb_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and skin lesions. This herbal preparation's anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis, including saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by reducing inflammation, inhibiting oxidative stress, and promoting wound healing. The benefits of Saponaria officinalis teas in treating dermatitis include reduced inflammation, accelerated healing, and improved skin health, making it a valuable natural remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Saponaria officinalis leaves.
Measure 2 cups of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Measure 2 tablespoons of honey and add it to the tea, stir well to dissolve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

To safely use Saponaria officinalis teas, it is recommended to start with small amounts, monitor your body's response, and discontinue use if any adverse reactions occur, especially if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus teas, also known as bilberry teas, have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The anthocyanins present in these teas are believed to help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to the treatment of dermatitis by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The benefits of using Vaccinium myrtillus teas to treat dermatitis include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and enhanced antioxidant defenses, making it a potentially valuable natural remedy for this skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Vaccinium myrtillus (blueberries) leaves and flowers.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces and add 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it to remove the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink 1 cup of the Vaccinium myrtillus tea 2-3 times a day for relief from dermatitis.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium myrtillus teas can be used as a potential treatment for dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications. To use Vaccinium myrtillus teas safely, individuals should be cautious of their allergic reactions, start with small doses to monitor their tolerance, and avoid consuming it if they have a history of kidney or liver problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Plantago lanceolata teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by redness, itching, and skin irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation, which are attributed to bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, thereby alleviating symptoms of dermatitis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Plantago lanceolata teas also help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and regeneration of skin tissue. The benefits of using Plantago lanceolata teas to treat dermatitis include reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the risk of skin infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from dermatitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Plantago lanceolata teas for dermatitis are not specifically mentioned, but teas made from Plantago sp. are traditionally used to treat skin problems such as eczema, wounds, and insect stings.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Plantago lanceolata teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal upset in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, monitor body reactions closely, and avoid taking it during pregnancy or breastfeeding due to lack of studies on its safety in these groups.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil, have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including terpinen-4-ol and cinnamaldehyde, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate the condition. By reducing inflammation and combating underlying infections, Melaleuca alternifolia teas help to alleviate symptoms of dermatitis, such as redness, itching, and swelling. Regular consumption of Melaleuca alternifolia teas has been shown to provide benefits in treating dermatitis, including reduced symptoms, improved skin health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves. You can use fresh or dried leaves, but fresh is better.
Chop the leaves into small pieces. This helps them release their oils and flavor.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot. Bring it to a boil, then turn off the heat.
Add the chopped Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the hot water. Let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup. Discard the leaves. Your Melaleuca alternifolia tea is now ready to drink.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Adverse drug reactions and toxicological reviews, Melaleuca alternifolia teas for dermatitis have not been mentioned in the provided study, however the study does mention the beneficial effects of Melaleuca alternifolia (also known as tea tree oil) in treating skin infections such as acne, herpes and scabies, but it does not provide information about teas specifically.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased heart rate in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, take precautions such as starting with small doses, monitoring your body's response, avoiding use if you have sensitive skin or allergies, and choosing high-quality teas from reputable sources.

preparations.herb_desc: Symphytum officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat various forms of dermatitis, including eczema and skin inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation, particularly the presence of allantoin and mucilages, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Symphytum officinale, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help to alleviate symptoms of dermatitis. Regular consumption of Symphytum officinale teas can lead to improved skin health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for treating dermatitis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Symphytum officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed to relieve dermatitis symptoms.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator and use it within 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Complementary therapies in medicine, Symphytum officinale teas for dermatitis show a positive effect on wound healing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Symphytum officinale teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should be cautious when using Symphytum officinale teas, especially if they have sensitive skin, and monitor their body's response to the treatment, avoiding excessive consumption and potential cross-reactions with other herbal preparations.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat dermatitis, an inflammatory skin condition, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and catechins present in these teas, such as quercetin and epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin. These bioactive constituents work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating the symptoms of dermatitis. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been shown to provide relief from dermatitis symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis tea leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the tea leaves in boiling water for 3-5 minutes.
Strain the tea leaves and discard them.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey with the tea, if desired, for sweetness.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe dermatitis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Acta pharmaceutica (Zagreb, Croatia), Camellia sinensis teas for dermatitis have been used historically, but more scientific evidence and standardised extracts are needed to confirm their clinical efficacy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat dermatitis, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, such as stomach cramps and diarrhea, and allergic reactions like hives or itching. To use Camellia sinensis teas safely, take precautions like starting with a small dose, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding use if you have sensitive skin or allergies, as well as keeping the tea out of reach of children.

preparation_study: According to Adverse drug reactions and toxicological reviews, Aloe barbadensis teas for dermatitis do not have any mentioned effects, however, the Aloe barbadensis plant has been found to have beneficial effects, specifically the Aloe vera, for healing wounds and burns.
intro_study: According to "The Australasian journal of dermatology", teas for dermatitis treatment, such as tea tree oil, appear to reduce symptomatology as effectively as tolnaftate 1% in the treatment of tinea pedis.
